# 🤖 Robotics (230)

AI is escaping the screen and grabbing things in the real world. These repos power robots that see, plan, and move, from vision-language-action models to the simulators where they learn before touching hardware.

[↑ Back to index](../README.md)

## VLA Models

Vision-language-action models that map what a robot sees and hears straight to motor commands

<details>
<summary>Show 33 repos</summary>

- [openvla/openvla](https://github.com/openvla/openvla) — ⭐ 6,285 · `Python` · Library — OpenVLA is an open-source vision-language-action model designed for robotic manipulation tasks, enabling robots to understand and act based on visual and linguistic input.
- [starVLA/starVLA](https://github.com/starVLA/starVLA) — ⭐ 2,418 · `Python` · Framework — StarVLA is a modular, "Lego-like" codebase for developing Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, allowing researchers to independently swap different vision-language or world-model backbones and action heads. It serves as a flexible framework for advancing robotic foundation models.
- [NVlabs/alpamayo](https://github.com/NVlabs/alpamayo) — ⭐ 1,824 · `Python` · Model Weights — Alpamayo 2 Super is a 32B parameter open reasoning Visual-Language-Action (VLA) model designed for Level 4 robotaxis. It enables self-driving vehicles to reason and explain their actions, outputting trajectories, meta-actions, and chain-of-causation.
- [Mininglamp-AI/Mano-P](https://github.com/Mininglamp-AI/Mano-P) — ⭐ 1,448 · AI Agent — An open-source GUI-VLA agent for edge devices like Apple M4 Macs, providing visual-driven, cross-platform GUI automation with local, on-device multi-step task planning and execution.
- [Robbyant/lingbot-va](https://github.com/Robbyant/lingbot-va) — ⭐ 1,240 · `Python` · AI Agent — This repository focuses on developing a causal video-action world model for generalist robot control, enabling robots to learn policies from internet videos.
- [X-Square-Robot/wall-x](https://github.com/X-Square-Robot/wall-x) — ⭐ 895 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains code for building general-purpose robots based on an embodied foundation model, serving as an implementation accompanying the 'Wall-OSS-0.5' technical report.
- [Tencent-Hunyuan/HY-Embodied](https://github.com/Tencent-Hunyuan/HY-Embodied) — ⭐ 807 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository presents "HY-Embodied," a project focused on embodied foundation models designed for real-world agents, likely involving visual language models (VLM) for robotic applications.
- [MIV-XJTU/FSDrive](https://github.com/MIV-XJTU/FSDrive) — ⭐ 727 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the official implementation for "FutureSightDrive," a project focused on visual chain-of-thought planning for autonomous driving agents. It enables autonomous vehicles to "think visually" using spatio-temporal CoT.
- [xiaomi-research/recogdrive](https://github.com/xiaomi-research/recogdrive) — ⭐ 532 · `Python` · Paper Code — This project presents ReCogDrive, a reinforced cognitive framework for end-to-end autonomous driving. It leverages Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to enhance cognitive capabilities and address complex challenges in autonomous driving scenarios.
- [Robbyant/lingbot-vla-v2](https://github.com/Robbyant/lingbot-vla-v2) — ⭐ 441 · `Python` · Library — This repository presents VLA 2.0, a Visual Language Action (VLA) model designed to unify robot control across various body types, from arms to dexterous hands. It uses a 55-dimensional 'spine' and a vast dataset to create a unified action space, enabling efficient deployment and control of robotic systems.
- [OpenDriveLab/WholebodyVLA](https://github.com/OpenDriveLab/WholebodyVLA) — ⭐ 436 · Framework — A research framework focused on unified latent Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models for whole-body loco-manipulation control in humanoid robots.
- [FluxVLA/FluxVLA](https://github.com/FluxVLA/FluxVLA) — ⭐ 413 · `Python` · Framework — FluxVLA is an all-in-one engineering platform for embodied AI, supporting the entire lifecycle from data to real-robot deployment using Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.
- [Hiangx-robotics/MetaFine](https://github.com/Hiangx-robotics/MetaFine) — ⭐ 303 · `Python` · Framework — MetaFine is a robotics evaluation framework designed to diagnose failure modes in robotic tasks. It breaks down evaluation into orthogonal dimensions like understanding, perception, and behavior, using VLA backbones and YAML-defined atomic skills for flexible task composition and research reproducibility.
- [IliaLarchenko/behavior-1k-solution](https://github.com/IliaLarchenko/behavior-1k-solution) — ⭐ 291 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the first-place solution for the 2025 BEHAVIOR Challenge. It presents a robotics solution, incorporating ideas from previous challenges and modifications specifically for Visual-Language-Action (VLA) tasks in home environments.
- [xiaomi-research/onevl](https://github.com/xiaomi-research/onevl) — ⭐ 287 · `Python` · Framework — A vision-language-action framework for autonomous driving, developed by Xiaomi Research. It integrates a bimodal auxiliary decoder with Qwen3-VL-4B to predict future scene frames and derive inference text from latent tokens, achieving high accuracy and low latency on benchmarks.
- [hzxie/DynamicVLA](https://github.com/hzxie/DynamicVLA) — ⭐ 268 · `Python` · Paper Code — This is the official implementation of DynamicVLA, a Vision-Language-Action model designed for dynamic object manipulation. The paper code won the Best Paper Award at the CVPR 2026 GigaBrain Workshop.
- [Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy-Embodied-0.5-VLA](https://github.com/Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy-Embodied-0.5-VLA) — ⭐ 218 · `Python` · Paper Code — A research project that explores Vision-Language-Action Models and provides a real-world robot learning stack for embodied AI applications.
- [xiaomi-research/unidrivevla](https://github.com/xiaomi-research/unidrivevla) — ⭐ 175 · `Python` · Paper Code — UniDriveVLA is a research project from Xiaomi that aims to unify understanding, perception, and action planning for autonomous driving. It integrates 3D perception with semantic reasoning to create a comprehensive driving system.
- [SJTU-DENG-Lab/WLA](https://github.com/SJTU-DENG-Lab/WLA) — ⭐ 117 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the official implementation of WLA-0, a World-Language-Action model designed for robots. It unifies world modeling, language reasoning, and action synthesis, allowing robots to plan actions based on textual, visual, and state inputs.
- [innovator-zero/FASTER](https://github.com/innovator-zero/FASTER) — ⭐ 115 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository introduces FASTER, a research project addressing the real-time responsiveness of robots in dynamic environments. It proposes a novel Horizon-Aware Schedule to accelerate robotic actions by compressing immediate action into a single denoising step, resulting in a 10x speed improvement for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.
- [snuvclab/dwm](https://github.com/snuvclab/dwm) — ⭐ 95 · `Python` · Paper Code — This project introduces Dexterous World Models (DWM), a scene-action-conditioned video diffusion model. It simulates human manipulation within static 3D environments, generating videos from egocentric hand motions.
- [mit-han-lab/foreact](https://github.com/mit-han-lab/foreact) — ⭐ 77 · `Python` · Paper Code — ForeAct is a research project, highlighted at CVPR 2026, that focuses on efficient visual foresight planning. It aims to steer Visual-Language Agents (VLAs) through atomic visual actions, particularly relevant for embodied AI applications.
- [kwai/MobileForge](https://github.com/kwai/MobileForge) — ⭐ 71 · `Python` · Paper Code — MobileForge is a system that enables AI agents to adapt to and navigate mobile GUI applications without requiring human labels or demonstrations. It uses hierarchical feedback-guided policy optimization for annotation-free adaptation, making it suitable for developing self-improving mobile GUI agents.
- [OpenHelix-Team/HiF-VLA](https://github.com/OpenHelix-Team/HiF-VLA) — ⭐ 60 · `Python` · Paper Code — HiF-VLA is an efficient, bidirectional spatiotemporal expansion Vision-Language-Action Model designed to enhance robotic capabilities. It allows robots to remember past actions and anticipate future tasks, using motion as a compact representation to achieve hindsight, insight, and foresight for mastering long-duration tasks.
- [alibaba-damo-academy/RynnWorld-4D](https://github.com/alibaba-damo-academy/RynnWorld-4D) — ⭐ 60 · `Python` · Paper Code — RynnWorld-4D presents 4D embodied world models designed for robotic manipulation tasks. It integrates advanced techniques like tri-branch DiT and cross-modal attention, trained on a large dataset of RGB frames with dense depth and optical flow.
- [zjunlp/LabVLA](https://github.com/zjunlp/LabVLA) — ⭐ 55 · `Python` · Paper Code — LabVLA is a project focused on developing Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models that can understand and perform experiments in scientific laboratory settings. It aims to bridge the gap between AI's ability to comprehend scientific knowledge and its capability to execute physical tasks in a lab environment.
- [SHAILAB-IPEC/EO1](https://github.com/SHAILAB-IPEC/EO1) — ⭐ 54 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Model Weights — EO-1 is an open-source unified embodied foundation model series, providing the core AI models that enable robotic systems to perform tasks. It serves as the foundational code for embodied AI applications.
- [pairlab/AMPLIFY](https://github.com/pairlab/AMPLIFY) — ⭐ 49 · `Python` · Paper Code — This is the official code implementation of AMPLIFY, a method designed for robot learning from videos by utilizing actionless motion priors. It enables robots to learn tasks more effectively by leveraging observational data.
- [jinyangwu/SEED](https://github.com/jinyangwu/SEED) — ⭐ 43 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the code implementation for SEED, a research project on self-evolutionary embodied diffusion models for robotic manipulation. It allows researchers to work with a model designed for visual-language-action tasks in robotics.
- [spacetools/SpaceTools](https://github.com/spacetools/SpaceTools) — ⭐ 28 · `Shell` · Paper Code — This repository contains code related to agentic spatial intelligence, focusing on enabling Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to perform spatial reasoning and real-robot manipulation. It utilizes vision and robotic tools, treating robots as adaptive tools for manipulation.
- [OpenBMB/MiniCPM-Robot](https://github.com/OpenBMB/MiniCPM-Robot) — ⭐ 26 · `Python` · Model Weights — MiniCPM-Robot is an open-source embodied AI model series designed to function as an on-device AI brain for robots, enabling them to understand, remember, and perform actions like manipulation.
- [HKU-MMLab/UniClawBench](https://github.com/HKU-MMLab/UniClawBench) — ⭐ 23 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the official code implementation for the UniClawBench project, a unified benchmark for evaluating Visual Language Models in robotic manipulation tasks.
- [TX-Leo/HumanEgo](https://github.com/TX-Leo/HumanEgo) — ⭐ 11 · `Python` · Framework — An open-source framework for zero-shot robot learning, enabling the training of robot policies from minutes of human egocentric videos.

</details>

## Simulation

Train policies in fast physics sandboxes before risking real hardware

<details>
<summary>Show 39 repos</summary>

- [Genesis-Embodied-AI/genesis-world](https://github.com/Genesis-Embodied-AI/genesis-world) — ⭐ 28,757 · `Python` · Framework — This repository provides 'Genesis World,' a generative environment and universal physics engine designed for general-purpose robotics and embodied AI research and learning.
- [google-deepmind/mujoco](https://github.com/google-deepmind/mujoco) — ⭐ 13,631 · `C++` · Library — MuJoCo is a general-purpose physics simulator designed for multi-joint dynamics with contact, widely used in robotics and machine learning research for tasks requiring realistic physical interaction.
- [isaac-sim/IsaacLab](https://github.com/isaac-sim/IsaacLab) — ⭐ 7,162 · `Python` · Framework — IsaacLab is a unified framework for robot learning built on NVIDIA Isaac Sim, designed to facilitate the development and training of robotic systems in GPU-accelerated simulation environments.
- [newton-physics/newton](https://github.com/newton-physics/newton) — ⭐ 4,855 · `Python` · Library — Newton is an open-source, GPU-accelerated physics simulation engine built on NVIDIA Warp, designed to assist roboticists and simulation researchers in running complex physics simulations.
- [cyberbotics/webots](https://github.com/cyberbotics/webots) — ⭐ 4,460 · `C++` · Application — An open-source robot simulator that provides a realistic 3D physics engine for education, research, and developing autonomous systems.
- [google-deepmind/mujoco\_menagerie](https://github.com/google-deepmind/mujoco_menagerie) — ⭐ 3,391 · `Python` · Dataset — This repository offers a curated collection of high-quality robot models for the MuJoCo physics engine, designed to support robotics research and simulation.
- [isaac-sim/IsaacSim](https://github.com/isaac-sim/IsaacSim) — ⭐ 3,343 · `Python` · Application — NVIDIA Isaac Sim is an open-source application built on NVIDIA Omniverse. It provides a platform for developing, simulating, and testing AI-driven robots within highly realistic virtual environments.
- [mujocolab/mjlab](https://github.com/mujocolab/mjlab) — ⭐ 2,231 · `Python` · Framework — mjlab is a GPU-accelerated robotics simulation framework (Isaac Lab API powered by MuJoCo-Warp) providing tools and an environment for reinforcement learning and robotics research.
- [davidmonterocrespo24/velxio](https://github.com/davidmonterocrespo24/velxio) — ⭐ 1,945 · `TypeScript` · Application — Velxio is a browser-based emulator that allows users to write, compile, and run code for 19 different embedded boards, including Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi, directly in their web browser without requiring physical hardware or cloud services.
- [RoboVerseOrg/RoboVerse](https://github.com/RoboVerseOrg/RoboVerse) — ⭐ 1,730 · `Python` · Framework — RoboVerse is a unified platform, dataset, and benchmark designed for scalable and generalizable robot learning. It facilitates cross-embodiment robot learning across multiple simulators, focusing on imitation and reinforcement learning.
- [Genesis-Embodied-AI/RoboGen](https://github.com/Genesis-Embodied-AI/RoboGen) — ⭐ 1,179 · `Python` · AI Agent — RoboGen is a generative and self-guided robotic agent that endlessly proposes and masters new skills. It focuses on generating diverse training tasks and environments for robots to learn within.
- [tddworks/baguette](https://github.com/tddworks/baguette) — ⭐ 1,099 · `Swift` · Application — This project provides a headless manager and farm for iOS simulators, enabling host-side input injection like taps, swipes, and multi-finger gestures, along with 60 fps streaming for remote access. It allows programmatic control and interaction with iOS simulators.
- [NVlabs/alpasim](https://github.com/NVlabs/alpasim) — ⭐ 1,041 · `Python` · Framework — An open-source simulation platform for autonomous vehicles, designed for the development and testing of end-to-end autonomous vehicle policies.
- [unitreerobotics/unifolm-world-model-action](https://github.com/unitreerobotics/unifolm-world-model-action) — ⭐ 995 · `Python` · Library — This repository contains a world model that predicts future interactions, designed for general-purpose robot learning applications.
- [SimWorld-AI/SimWorld](https://github.com/SimWorld-AI/SimWorld) — ⭐ 640 · `Python` · Application — SimWorld is an open-ended, realistic simulator designed for autonomous agents operating in complex physical and social environments. It provides a platform for developing and testing agent behaviors and navigation.
- [unilabsim/UniLab](https://github.com/unilabsim/UniLab) — ⭐ 604 · `Python` · Framework — UniLab is a heterogeneous architecture for robot reinforcement learning that aims to go beyond GPU-dominant paradigms. It provides a system-level organization for training stacks in robotics RL, offering a different architectural approach for underlying distribution of labor.
- [NVlabs/mimicgen](https://github.com/NVlabs/mimicgen) — ⭐ 596 · `Python` · Library — This repository contains simulation environments developed for the MimicGen project, used to generate robot training data from human demonstrations.
- [NVIDIA/GR00T-Dreams](https://github.com/NVIDIA/GR00T-Dreams) — ⭐ 548 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Library — DreamGen is an NVIDIA project aimed at solving the robotics data problem by generating synthetic training data for robots using world models, often from single images and text prompts.
- [intrinsic-dev/aic](https://github.com/intrinsic-dev/aic) — ⭐ 433 · `Python` · Library — A toolkit designed for the AI for Industry Challenge, providing simulations of industrial robotic manipulation tasks specifically for AI training and development.
- [URLab-Sim/UnrealRoboticsLab](https://github.com/URLab-Sim/UnrealRoboticsLab) — ⭐ 391 · `C++` · Framework — A high-fidelity, open-source robotics simulator integrating Unreal Engine's rendering with MuJoCo's physics engine, providing a robust environment for robotics research and development.
- [discoverse-dev/gs\_playground](https://github.com/discoverse-dev/gs_playground) — ⭐ 352 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Application — A photorealistic robot learning simulator that leverages 3D Gaussian Splatting for realistic scene rendering. - [NVlabs/sage](https://github.com/NVlabs/sage) — ⭐ 282 · `Python` · Paper Code — SAGE is an official code release for a scalable agentic system designed to generate 3D scenes, primarily intended for embodied AI training.
- [ttktjmt/mjswan](https://github.com/ttktjmt/mjswan) — ⭐ 277 · `Python` · Framework — This project offers a framework for running MuJoCo simulations on WebAssembly, integrating neural networks for robotics and reinforcement learning. It enables packaging MuJoCo scenes and replaying complex motions directly within a web browser.
- [MuammerBay/isaac\_so\_arm101](https://github.com/MuammerBay/isaac_so_arm101) — ⭐ 246 · `Python` · Application — This project provides an external integration for Isaac Lab to train SO-ARM100/101 arm robots using reinforcement learning. It's designed for robotic simulation and control experiments.
- [Axellwppr/motion\_tracking](https://github.com/Axellwppr/motion_tracking) — ⭐ 244 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides training code for a motion tracking policy, demonstrated with a GentleHumanoid in a MuJoCo simulation environment. It enables the replay of motions and execution of tracking policies, with potential for browser-based simulation.
- [PointsCoder/OpenReal2Sim](https://github.com/PointsCoder/OpenReal2Sim) — ⭐ 214 · `Python` · Library — OpenReal2Sim is a Python toolbox designed for reconstructing real-world assets and integrating them into robotic simulation environments. - [DexForce/EmbodiChain](https://github.com/DexForce/EmbodiChain) — ⭐ 165 · `Python` · Framework — EmbodiChain is a GPU-accelerated, modular platform designed for building and training generalized embodied AI agents. - [Insta360-Research-Team/AirSim360](https://github.com/Insta360-Research-Team/AirSim360) — ⭐ 139 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the official implementation of AirSim360, a panoramic simulation platform designed for generating drone view datasets using Unreal Engine 5.
- [valeoai/V-Max](https://github.com/valeoai/V-Max) — ⭐ 139 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This repository provides a plug-and-play extension for the Waymax simulator, designed to facilitate learning-based motion planning in simulated autonomous driving environments.
- [mekion/the-bimo-project](https://github.com/mekion/the-bimo-project) — ⭐ 135 · `Python` · Framework — Bimo is an open-source bipedal robotics platform. It includes a Python API and an Isaac Lab environment for simulation, designed for sim-to-real transfer, and is fully 3D printable.
- [learnsyslab/crazyflow](https://github.com/learnsyslab/crazyflow) — ⭐ 114 · `Python` · Framework — A scalable simulation framework for drones, utilizing the JAX library to provide efficient and high-performance computational capabilities.
- [Genesis-Embodied-AI/quadrants](https://github.com/Genesis-Embodied-AI/quadrants) — ⭐ 90 · `C++` · Framework — A high-performance, multi-platform compiler designed for physics simulations, specifically optimized for robotics applications like the Genesis World simulation, addressing the bottleneck of real-world physical testing.
- [asu-iris/comfree\_warp](https://github.com/asu-iris/comfree_warp) — ⭐ 74 · `Python` · Library — This is a GPU-parallel analytical physics engine compatible with the Mujoco API. It is designed for simulating contact-rich robotics tasks.
- [spikelab-jhu/isaac-sim-event-camera-plugin](https://github.com/spikelab-jhu/isaac-sim-event-camera-plugin) — ⭐ 69 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This repository provides a plugin for Isaac Sim that simulates high-rate event camera data, utilizing motion-vector frame interpolation for acceleration. ## Control

Closed-loop policies that turn high-level intent into precise joint motion

<details>
<summary>Show 67 repos</summary>

- [commaai/openpilot](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot) — ⭐ 61,001 · `Python` · Application — openpilot is an operating system for robotics that enhances advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) in over 300 supported car models.
- [AtsushiSakai/PythonRobotics](https://github.com/AtsushiSakai/PythonRobotics) — ⭐ 29,449 · `Python` · Tutorial/Course — This repository offers Python sample code and a textbook for a wide range of robotics algorithms, including SLAM, path planning, and localization for autonomous vehicles. It serves as an educational resource for practical robotics implementations.
- [CursorTouch/Windows-MCP](https://github.com/CursorTouch/Windows-MCP) — ⭐ 5,668 · `Python` · MCP Server — This is an MCP (Multimodal Control Protocol) server designed to enable AI agents to control and interact with Windows computers. It provides the necessary interface for agents to automate desktop tasks and engage with the Windows operating system.
- [memovai/mimiclaw](https://github.com/memovai/mimiclaw) — ⭐ 5,478 · `C` · Agent Framework — MimiClaw enables running the OpenClaw AI assistant on low-cost embedded hardware like an ESP32-S3 chip, eliminating the need for traditional operating systems or powerful devices.
- [real-stanford/diffusion\_policy](https://github.com/real-stanford/diffusion_policy) — ⭐ 4,205 · `Python` · Paper Code — Diffusion Policy implements visuomotor policy learning using action diffusion models, enabling robots to learn complex behaviors from visual inputs, as presented at RSS 2023.
- [Seeed-Projects/reBot-DevArm](https://github.com/Seeed-Projects/reBot-DevArm) — ⭐ 3,373 · Application — This repository provides an open-source robotic arm, including its complete hardware designs and a full software stack, intended for developers to build and utilize robotic systems.

- [hrh6666/Flexible-Locomotion-Learning-with-Diffusion-Model-Predictive-Control](https://github.com/hrh6666/Flexible-Locomotion-Learning-with-Diffusion-Model-Predictive-Control) — ⭐ 19 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the official implementation of a diffusion-planning framework for flexible locomotion learning, specifically designed for real-world quadruped robots. It uses Diffusion Model Predictive Control to enable adaptable robot movement, moving beyond fixed RL policies or hand-crafted dynamics.
